methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Collaborative Robotics, founded by Brad Porter, is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company currently valued at $500M+ post-money, having raised $150M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five same-stage, same-sector private peers (1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Fairmat, and GrayMatter Robotics) alongside one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, anchoring to the $500M last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $950M. The peer-median method across the five private comparables yields $1.6B, with the peer set ranging from $300M (GrayMatter Robotics, Fairmat) to $10.0B (1X Technologies). The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and market positioning within the robotics sector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $808M to $1.9B, with a median of $1.3B. The current $500M valuation sits at the lower bound of this range, suggesting meaningful upside if the company executes to Series C expectations. Key uncertainties include the appropriate weighting of 1X Technologies' $10B outlier valuation and the extent to which Collaborative Robotics' technology differentiation and market traction justify positioning within the upper or lower quartile of peers.
